summary: DHH announces Rails 0.8, the biggest release yet with nearly 100 additions and changes after fifty days of development. The release includes a new email framework and numerous smaller improvements. He highlights the growing community, with 23 contributors submitting patches, and expresses pride in Rails becoming a genuine community movement rather than a solo project.
key_points: Rails 0.8 is the largest release to date with nearly 100 additions, changes, tweaks, and fixes
A new framework for sending email is the headline feature
23 different contributors submitted patches, marking the highest contributor count yet
Rails is transitioning from a solo project to a community-driven movement
Installation is streamlined through RubyGems with automatic dependency management
money_quotes: It's been fifty days since our last confession, so it's no wonder that this outpouring is by far the biggest yet in Rails history.
This is truely turning into a community movement and I'm extremely pleased to be the steward for it.
I've counted at least 23 different people with patches in Rails 0.8 — and there are many, many more responsible for suggestions and bug reports.
key_insight: Rails 0.8 marks the moment Rails began transforming from DHH's personal framework into a genuine open source community project, with 23 contributors signaling the early growth of what would become one of the most influential web frameworks.
